RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance

Dear Mr. Walker:
QUESTION
CONCLUSION
This letter is-in response to your request for advice regarding the reporting
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Nothing in this=letter shoulcl be
construed to evaluate any conduct that may have already taken place, and anv
conclusions contained in this letter apply only to prospectiv. ,"iio;j;

letter is based on the facts presented- The Fair potiticat practices Commission (the
"commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders assistance. (In re oglesby
(1975) 1 FPPC ops'-71.) Because your lett.er seeks general information, we are treating
your request as one for informal assistance.l
Does the Act impose any reporting obligations on an organizatron that has
received a total of $750 in donations, or any p.ohibitiors on hoiv that money is spentz
No' Based upon the facts you provided in your letter, the organization does not
qualify as a recipient committee because it has not received contribritions of $1,000 or

more in a calendar year. Until such time as it meets either of those thresholds, you are
not subject to the registration or reporting provisions of the Act.
You are a member of a non-chartered Democratic club (the .,club,). The club
received $250 before the November 7,2006 election from a state senator. After the
election, the club received $500 from a. state assembly member. The ctub has spent
$250 on creatinglnternet websites, buying coffee ana aonuts_for volunteers, and printing
flyers' The club has not spent the remaining $500. rhe funas were not spent for a
particular candidate, but for a"getout the vJte" drive forlocal Democrats. The club is
considering contributing the $500 to a committee for "california for John Edwards
2008;,
In our telephone conversation on January 17, 2007,you stated that you received
no other funds in2o06,had a small collection oipeopl;;h" participated in the club, did
not collect dues, and have nol filed papers of any kind to be a recognized club. The club
is in its inception phases, and its pu.por", you stated, is to ..get out the vote,, to the local
Latino and African-American poputations who historically have had a low voter-turnout.
FACTS
ANALYSIS
.. 
The campaign disclosure provisions of the Act require candidates and
"committees" to file periodic t"po.t. disclosing "o"t iurlions received and expenditures
made' (section 84100 et seq-) The term "committee" includes a person or combination
of persons that receiv"r 
"ori.ibrrtions totaling $ 1,000 or more in a carend ar year (a
"recipient committee,). (Sectio n g201 3 (a). Jopy enclor"J.;
Section 82015 defines the term .,contribution,, as a pa5rment made for political
purposes' A payment is made for political purposes if it is made ,,for the purplse of
influencing or attempting to influence the action of the,rot"r, for or against the
nomination or election of a candidate or candidates, or the qualification or passage of any
measure" or is "received by or made at the behest of u 
"andidate or committee.,,
(Regulation lS2ts(a)(t) and e).)
Any group that receives contributions of $1,000 or more in a calend,aryear meets
the definition of "committee" and becomes subjectio the Act,s reporting requirements.
(Section 82013(a).)
Under the facts you presented, the club does not qualify as a committee under the
Act because it has not met the $ I ,000 threshold found in section g201 3 (a). Thus, the

If in coming years, the Club accepts $ 1,000 or more in contributions in a calend.ar
year it would be subject to the registration urra."po.ti.rg.-"qri."-ents under the Act.5
while the club does not meet the minimum thresholdslt this time, it wilr be important
for you to closery track your contributions and expendifures.
Finally, with respect-to the organization's $500 potential contribution to a

candidate and the commission does not regulate federal campaigns, there is nothing in
the Act that would prohibit you from making this coniibrii";. ?ou.uy *uri,o inquire
with the Federal Election Commissio, o, tlri, point, ho*.v"r.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether an organization that has received $750 in donations is subject to reporting obligations and restrictions on spending under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The organization does not qualify as a recipient committee under the Act because it has not received $1,000 or more in contributions in a calendar year, and therefore is not subject to reporting requirements at this time. However, the organization must closely track contributions and expenditures going forward, as it would become subject to the Act's requirements once it meets the $1,000 threshold.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; Skipped (too short): facts, question; QUESTION has only 0 words; FACTS has only 0 words; LLM: Document contains clear QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. Parsing notes indicated these sections were skipped as 'too short' but they are actually present and substantive. File number I-07-008 indicates informal advice letter. Document dated January 25, 2007.

embedding.summary: FPPC informal advice letter regarding whether a non-chartered Democratic club that received $750 in donations must comply with Political Reform Act reporting provisions. The FPPC concludes the club does not qualify as a recipient committee and is not subject to reporting requirements until it receives $1,000 or more in contributions in a calendar year.
